([Jawi](/wiki/Jawi_script "Jawi script"): المهتدي بالله ابن حسن البلقية;[[1]](#cite_note-Senior-1) born 17 February 1974) is the eldest son of [Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Sultan_Hassanal_Bolkiah "Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah") and his wife [Queen Saleha](/wiki/Queen_Saleha_of_Brunei "Queen Saleha of Brunei").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He is the [Crown Prince](/wiki/Crown_Prince "Crown Prince") of [Brunei Darussalam](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"), and is first in the [line of succession to the Bruneian throne](/wiki/Succession_to_the_Bruneian_throne "Succession to the Bruneian throne"). He is expected to eventually lead the state and government, and is regarded as the second most significant figure in the political hierarchy.[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Heir apparent to the Bruneian throne (born 1974)

In this [Malay name](/wiki/Malaysian_names#Malay_names "Malaysian names"), the name Hassanal Bolkiah is a [patronymic](/wiki/Patronymic "Patronymic"), and the person should be referred to by his [given name](/wiki/Given_name "Given name"), Al-Muhtadee Billah.

**Al-Muhtadee Billah ibni Hassanal Bolkiah** ([Jawi](/wiki/Jawi_script "Jawi script"): المهتدي بالله ابن حسن البلقية;[[1]](#cite_note-Senior-1) born 17 February 1974) is the eldest son of [Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Sultan_Hassanal_Bolkiah "Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah") and his wife [Queen Saleha](/wiki/Queen_Saleha_of_Brunei "Queen Saleha of Brunei").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He is the [Crown Prince](/wiki/Crown_Prince "Crown Prince") of [Brunei Darussalam](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"), and is first in the [line of succession to the Bruneian throne](/wiki/Succession_to_the_Bruneian_throne "Succession to the Bruneian throne"). He is expected to eventually lead the state and government, and is regarded as the second most significant figure in the political hierarchy.[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Al-Muhtadee_Billah&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Prince Muda (Prince) Al-Muhtadee Billah was born at [Istana Darul Hana](/wiki/Istana_Darul_Hana "Istana Darul Hana"), [Bandar Seri Begawan](/wiki/Bandar_Seri_Begawan "Bandar Seri Begawan") during the reign of his father on 17 February 1974.[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4) He is the first-born son, and thus heir to the [throne of Brunei](/wiki/List_of_Sultans_of_Brunei "List of Sultans of Brunei"). His early schooling was at the Putera Puteri School at Istana Darul Hana.[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4) He received his primary education at [St. Andrew's School](/wiki/St._Andrew%27s_School,_Brunei "St. Andrew's School, Brunei").[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4) He finished reading the "surah-surah Lazim" and the [Quran](/wiki/Quran "Quran") in 1988.[[5]](#cite_note-:2-5) Subsequently, he progressed through the Brunei Junior Certificate of Education (BJCE) in 1988, and the [General Certificate of Education](/wiki/General_Certificate_of_Education "General Certificate of Education") (GCE) [Ordinary Level](/wiki/GCE_Ordinary_Level "GCE Ordinary Level") exam in 1991 while he was studying at the [Paduka Seri Begawan Sultan Science College](/wiki/Paduka_Seri_Begawan_Sultan_Science_College "Paduka Seri Begawan Sultan Science College").[[5]](#cite_note-:2-5) He was further educated at [Emanuel School](/wiki/Emanuel_School "Emanuel School") in London.[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4) He passed his [GCE Advanced Level](/wiki/GCE_Advanced_Level "GCE Advanced Level") exams in 1994.[[5]](#cite_note-:2-5)

Al-Muhtadee Billah attended tutorials at the [Universiti Brunei Darussalam](/wiki/Universiti_Brunei_Darussalam "Universiti Brunei Darussalam") (UBD), and began his overseas education at the [Oxford Centre for Islamic Studies](/wiki/Oxford_Centre_for_Islamic_Studies "Oxford Centre for Islamic Studies") in October 1995.[[6]](#cite_note-PMO-6)[[5]](#cite_note-:2-5) He matriculated for admission to [University of Oxford](/wiki/University_of_Oxford "University of Oxford")'s Foreign Service Programme at [Magdalen College, Oxford](/wiki/Magdalen_College,_Oxford "Magdalen College, Oxford"), where he graduated in 1997.[[6]](#cite_note-PMO-6) During his two years at Oxford, the heir was known as "Mr. Omar Hassan,"[[7]](#cite_note-7) allowing him to live anonymously and enjoy a typical student social life.[[8]](#cite_note-8) While at Oxford, he followed a programme of study specially designed for him involving [Islamic studies](/wiki/Islamic_studies "Islamic studies"), trade, diplomacy, and [international relations](/wiki/International_relations "International relations"). He received his [diploma](/wiki/Diploma "Diploma") in [Diplomatics](/wiki/Diplomatics "Diplomatics") in a special convocation held on 3 August 1998 in Bandar Seri Begawan.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Crown Prince

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Al-Muhtadee_Billah&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Crown Prince")]

### Early appointments and duties

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Al-Muhtadee_Billah&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early appointments and duties")]

Al-Muhtadee Billah was proclaimed [Crown Prince of Brunei](/wiki/Crown_Prince_of_Brunei "Crown Prince of Brunei") on 10 August 1998 at the [Istana Nurul Iman](/wiki/Istana_Nurul_Iman "Istana Nurul Iman").[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11) At the ceremony, his father, the Sultan of Brunei, bestowed upon him the 'Keris Si Naga'.[[12]](#cite_note-12) This conferred him in line to become the 30th [Sultan of Brunei](/wiki/Sultan_of_Brunei "Sultan of Brunei"). The ceremony was followed by the procession around the capital, Bandar Seri Begawan.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

The [Public Service Department](/wiki/Public_Service_Department_(Brunei) "Public Service Department (Brunei)"), Public Service Commission, Immigration Department, Labor Department, [Ministry of Finance](/wiki/Ministry_of_Finance_and_Economy_(Brunei) "Ministry of Finance and Economy (Brunei)"), [Ministry of Health](/wiki/Ministry_of_Health_(Brunei) "Ministry of Health (Brunei)"), [Ministry of Communications](/wiki/Ministry_of_Transport_and_Infocommunications "Ministry of Transport and Infocommunications"), [Ministry of Culture, Youth and Sports](/wiki/Ministry_of_Culture,_Youth_and_Sports_(Brunei) "Ministry of Culture, Youth and Sports (Brunei)"), [Ministry of Religious Affairs](/wiki/Ministry_of_Religious_Affairs_(Brunei) "Ministry of Religious Affairs (Brunei)"), and [Datastream Technology Communications](/wiki/Datastream_Digital "Datastream Digital") (DST) are just a few of the government agencies that Al-Muhtadee Billah has been attached to while he prepares to become the future head of state and head of statehood. The purpose of the attachments is to provide him with as much exposure to the nation's operations as possible.[[5]](#cite_note-:2-5)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a0/C-130J_circumnavigates_globe.jpg/250px-C-130J_circumnavigates_globe.jpg)](/wiki/File:C-130J_circumnavigates_globe.jpg)

Al-Muhtadee Billah visiting a [C-130J Hercules](/wiki/C-130J_Hercules "C-130J Hercules") in 2005

Al-Muhtadee Billah also holds positions as a [general](/wiki/General_officer "General officer") in the [Royal Brunei Armed Forces](/wiki/Royal_Brunei_Armed_Forces "Royal Brunei Armed Forces") (RBAF) since 2004,[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) the deputy [inspector general](/wiki/Inspector_general "Inspector general") of the [Royal Brunei Police Force](/wiki/Royal_Brunei_Police_Force "Royal Brunei Police Force") (RBPF) since 2005,[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) and senior minister in the [Prime Minister's Office](/wiki/Prime_Minister%27s_Office_(Brunei) "Prime Minister's Office (Brunei)") (PMO) since 24 May 2005.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[5]](#cite_note-:2-5)[[15]](#cite_note-:6-15) He gives audiences to foreign [ambassadors](/wiki/Ambassador "Ambassador") and [high commissioners](/wiki/High_commissioner "High commissioner"),[[16]](#cite_note-16) acts as deputy sultan while his father is away, and attends more formal events in order to improve his public speaking skills when giving *sabda* (crown prince royal speeches).[[17]](#cite_note-17) He also serves as the [pro-chancellor](/wiki/Pro-chancellor "Pro-chancellor") of [Universiti Brunei Darussalam](/wiki/Universiti_Brunei_Darussalam "Universiti Brunei Darussalam") (UBD) since 16 September 1998;[[18]](#cite_note-:8-18) [Universiti Islam Sultan Sharif Ali](/wiki/Universiti_Islam_Sultan_Sharif_Ali "Universiti Islam Sultan Sharif Ali") (UNISSA); and [Universiti Teknologi Brunei](/wiki/Universiti_Teknologi_Brunei "Universiti Teknologi Brunei") (UTB).[[15]](#cite_note-:6-15)

In the [2005 Bruneian cabinet reshuffle](/wiki/2005_Bruneian_cabinet_reshuffle "2005 Bruneian cabinet reshuffle") on 24 May, Al-Muhtadee Billah was appointed senior minister at the [Prime Minister's Office](/wiki/Prime_Minister%27s_Office_(Brunei) "Prime Minister's Office (Brunei)").[[19]](#cite_note-:13-19)[[20]](#cite_note-:02-20) Additionally, he is the [chairman](/wiki/Chairman "Chairman") of the [board of directors](/wiki/Board_of_directors "Board of directors") for Brunei Shell Joint Venture (BSJV) Companies from 2018 to 2024, the chairman of the [Wawasan Brunei Supreme Council](/wiki/Wawasan_Brunei_2035 "Wawasan Brunei 2035") since 2014, the chairman of [Autoriti Monetari Brunei Darussalam](/wiki/Autoriti_Monetari_Brunei_Darussalam "Autoriti Monetari Brunei Darussalam") (AMBD) from 2011 to 2020, and the royal [patron](/wiki/Patron "Patron") of the [Heart of Borneo](/wiki/Heart_of_Borneo "Heart of Borneo") National Council in Brunei since 2011. In addition, he has served as the National Disaster Management Council's (NDMC) chairman from 2006 to 2018, the chairman of the Manpower Planning Council from 2015 to 2019, the [chief scout](/wiki/Chief_scout "Chief scout") of the [Persekutuan Pengakap Negara Brunei Darussalam](/wiki/Persekutuan_Pengakap_Negara_Brunei_Darussalam "Persekutuan Pengakap Negara Brunei Darussalam") (PPNBD) from 2013 to 2018,[[21]](#cite_note-21) and the patron of the [Temburong District](/wiki/Temburong_District "Temburong District") Development Authority since 2018.[[15]](#cite_note-:6-15)

### Engagements in foreign relations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Al-Muhtadee_Billah&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Engagements in foreign relations")]

An annual highlight of the Singapore–Brunei Young Leaders' Programme (YLP) was Al-Muhtadee Billah's state visits to Singapore.[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23)[[24]](#cite_note-:5-24) 2019 sees him meet dignitaries and state officials, including [President](/wiki/President_of_Singapore "President of Singapore") [Halimah Yacob](/wiki/Halimah_Yacob "Halimah Yacob") and [Prime Minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Singapore "Prime Minister of Singapore") [Lee Hsien Loong](/wiki/Lee_Hsien_Loong "Lee Hsien Loong").[[24]](#cite_note-:5-24) These visits also solidify the two countries' close relationship.[[25]](#cite_note-25) He also paid visits to government buildings and notable locations, including [Changi Naval Base](/wiki/Changi_Naval_Base "Changi Naval Base"), [Our Tampines Hub](/wiki/Our_Tampines_Hub "Our Tampines Hub"), and [Victoria School](/wiki/Victoria_School "Victoria School").[[26]](#cite_note-26)[[24]](#cite_note-:5-24)

While expressing satisfaction with the [United Nations](/wiki/United_Nations "United Nations")' (UN) efforts, Al-Muhtadee Billah underlined the need for structural changes to reflect the globalised world during the [general debate of the sixty-seventh session of the UN General Assembly](/wiki/General_debate_of_the_sixty-seventh_session_of_the_United_Nations_General_Assembly "General debate of the sixty-seventh session of the United Nations General Assembly"). He emphasised the significance of reaching a regional agreement, using the ASEAN's strategy as an illustration. He also listed three major areas in which the UN is essential to building a brighter future that is marked by optimism, assurance, and trust for everyone.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/7/71/Minister_for_Foreign_Affairs_Kishida_Pays_Courtesy_Call_on_His_Royal_Highness_Crown_Prince_Al-Muhtadee_Billah_01.jpg)](/wiki/File:Minister_for_Foreign_Affairs_Kishida_Pays_Courtesy_Call_on_His_Royal_Highness_Crown_Prince_Al-Muhtadee_Billah_01.jpg)

[Fumio Kishida](/wiki/Fumio_Kishida "Fumio Kishida") meeting Al-Muhtadee Billah in 2013

Al-Muhtadee Billah was awarded the title of Honorary Member of Special Forces Regiment, RBAF on 4 November 2013, in recognition of his leadership, involvement, and contributions to the growth of the RBAF since his father had conferred the rank of General of the RBAF on him on 25 March 2004.[[28]](#cite_note-Borneo_Bulletin-28) Later on 13 November 2013, he visited the Sri Iskandar Camp, [Johor](/wiki/Johor "Johor") and received an honorary [Green Beret](/wiki/Gerak_Khas_(Malaysian_Army_unit) "Gerak Khas (Malaysian Army unit)"). General [Zulkifeli Mohd Zin](/wiki/Zulkifeli_Mohd_Zin "Zulkifeli Mohd Zin") gave the award during a ceremony held on the parade area of the camp.[[29]](#cite_note-:12-29) He gave the Chief of the [21st Special Service Group](/wiki/21st_Special_Service_Group "21st Special Service Group") (21 GGK) a plaque, and he was then given a [guard of honour](/wiki/Guard_of_honour "Guard of honour") at the 21 GGK Parade Square.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

On 3 May 2014, Al-Muhtadee Billah paid a visit to Indonesia's Special Forces Command ([Kopassus](/wiki/Kopassus "Kopassus")), where he was bestowed with the title of Honorary Member. In a *sabda*, he acknowledged the [strong bilateral ties between Brunei and Indonesia](/wiki/Brunei%E2%80%93Indonesia_relations "Brunei–Indonesia relations"), particularly in the area of defense cooperation, and thanked Kopassus for the invitation during the audience. He went to the meeting room where Major General Agus Sutomo was giving a briefing.[[31]](#cite_note-:9-31) Al-Muhtadee Billah was met with a guard of honour upon arriving at [Kesatrian Marinir Hartono](/wiki/South_Jakarta "South Jakarta") on 6 May 2014, together with Admiral [Marsetio](/wiki/Marsetio "Marsetio") and Major General A. Faridz Washington. He first attended a conferment ceremony when he was named an honorary member of the [Indonesian Marine Corps](/wiki/Indonesian_Marine_Corps "Indonesian Marine Corps"), followed by an honorary beret and badge from Major General Faridz.[[32]](#cite_note-:11-32)

[Deputy Prime Minister](/wiki/Deputy_Prime_Minister_of_Singapore "Deputy Prime Minister of Singapore") [Teo Chee Hean](/wiki/Teo_Chee_Hean "Teo Chee Hean") invited Al-Muhtadee Billah to come to Singapore for a five-day official visit as part of the Singapore–Brunei YLP from 1 to 5 September 2014. In addition to driving the improved [Light Strike Vehicle](/wiki/Light_Strike_Vehicle_(Singapore) "Light Strike Vehicle (Singapore)") (LSV) Mark-II and witnessing live shooting displays, he received the SAF's Honorary Advanced Combat Skills Badge during his visit. His visit highlights the unique and established defence cooperation between Brunei and Singapore, which is emphasised by frequent encounters through visits, bilateral exercises, and professional exchanges.[[33]](#cite_note-:10-33)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ef/The_Vice_President%2C_Shri_M._Hamid_Ansari_and_Smt._Salma_Ansari_with_His_Royal_Highness_Crown_Prince_Haji_Al-Muhtadee_Billah_and_Her_Royal_Highness_Pengiran_Anak_Isteri_Pengiran_Anak_Sarah%2C_in_Brunei_on_February_02%2C_2016.jpg/250px-thumbnail.jpg)](/wiki/File:The_Vice_President,_Shri_M._Hamid_Ansari_and_Smt._Salma_Ansari_with_His_Royal_Highness_Crown_Prince_Haji_Al-Muhtadee_Billah_and_Her_Royal_Highness_Pengiran_Anak_Isteri_Pengiran_Anak_Sarah,_in_Brunei_on_February_02,_2016.jpg)

[Mohammad Hamid Ansari](/wiki/Mohammad_Hamid_Ansari "Mohammad Hamid Ansari") with Al-Muhtadee Billah and Pengiran Anak Sarah in 2016

Al-Muhtadee Billah stated during the NDMC meeting on 10 October 2016, that a nationwide strategy is required to stop the [Zika virus](/wiki/Zika_virus "Zika virus") from spreading since public support for government initiatives depends on shared responsibility. He underlined that for government initiatives like fogging and fumigation to be successful, the community must be actively involved. He thanked [Allah](/wiki/Allah "Allah") for sparing Brunei from the [global health crisis](/wiki/2015%E2%80%9316_Zika_virus_epidemic "2015–16 Zika virus epidemic") and emphasised the council's responsibility in educating the people and fostering readiness.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

In honour of the 50th anniversary of the Currency Interchangeability Agreement (CIA), Al-Muhtadee Billah and Teo Chee Hean launched a special exhibition named "Abode of Peace & the Lion City: A Brunei-Singapore Exhibition" has opened at the art gallery at the [Dermaga Diraja Bandar Seri Begawan](/wiki/Dermaga_Diraja_Bandar_Seri_Begawan "Dermaga Diraja Bandar Seri Begawan") on 4 November 2017. The exhibition in Bandar Seri Begawan, and it features coins, notes, and stamps that mark the milestone. The Ministry of Culture, Youth, and Sports of Brunei and the [Singapore Philatelic Museum](/wiki/Singapore_Philatelic_Museum "Singapore Philatelic Museum") collaborated on the two-part exhibition's curation.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

Al-Muhtadee Billah highlighted the need of cooperation between the public and private sectors in discovering economic possibilities and assisting firms in navigating the [COVID-19 epidemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic_in_Brunei "COVID-19 pandemic in Brunei") during the recent ASEAN Business Awards (ABA) 2021 opening session, which was conducted via video recording. He underlined how crucial the private sector has been to the region's economic recovery and stressed the value of initiatives like the ABA, the ASEAN Business and Investment Summit, and the ASEAN Business Roundtables in promoting cooperation between the public and private sectors. He also mentioned how quickly developing technologies are changing the face of the economy.[[36]](#cite_note-36)

## Charity work

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Al-Muhtadee_Billah&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Charity work")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a3/2023_Majlis_Ramah_Mesra_Belait_82.jpg/250px-2023_Majlis_Ramah_Mesra_Belait_82.jpg)](/wiki/File:2023_Majlis_Ramah_Mesra_Belait_82.jpg)

Al-Muhtadee Billah on his 2023 visit to the [Belait District](/wiki/Belait_District "Belait District"), seen greeting the populace[[37]](#cite_note-37)

The Dana Pengiran Muda Mahkota Al-Muhtadee Billah for Orphans (DANA) Act (Chapter 185)[[38]](#cite_note-38) commanded Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah to create the DANA. Al-Muhtadee Billah has given permission for the DANA to be introduced at the [Jerudong Polo Club](/w/index.php?title=Jerudong_Polo_Club&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jerudong Polo Club (page does not exist)") on 25 August 1998.[[39]](#cite_note-:7-39) The formal presentation and launch of the DANA Account, which happened at the Berakas International Convention Center (ICC) on 30 August 1999.[[40]](#cite_note-40) Various government and private sector organisations, financial institutions, business and industry associations, groups, and private people have made their contributions to the DANA before Al-Muhtadee Billah in connection with the DANA's debut.[[39]](#cite_note-:7-39)[[41]](#cite_note-41)[[42]](#cite_note-42) Three contributors gave [B$](/wiki/B$ "B$")75,381 to DANA on 2 April 2024, during a ceremony held in the multipurpose hall of the Yayasan Sultan Haji Hassanal Bolkiah Complex.[[43]](#cite_note-43)

In honour of [Hari Raya Aidilfitri](/wiki/Hari_Raya_Aidilfitri "Hari Raya Aidilfitri"), Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah has given his permission to once again provide the His Majesty's Personal Gifts (Kurnia) to 4,651 beneficiaries countrywide who are [special needs](/wiki/Special_needs "Special needs"), [orphans](/wiki/Orphans "Orphans"), the poor, and beneficiaries of Monthly Welfare Assistance.[[44]](#cite_note-44) The presentation of the personal gifts by the Sultan's sons, acting as his personal representatives, kicked off the ceremony. This was followed by the simultaneous distribution at many locations across all four districts. Al-Muhtadee Billah agreed to give the individual presents to a several thousand recipients from the districts at every yearly events.[[45]](#cite_note-45)[[46]](#cite_note-46)[[47]](#cite_note-47)

### Interests

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b5/Family_of_Crown_Prince_Al-Muhtadee_Billah_in_2024.jpg/250px-Family_of_Crown_Prince_Al-Muhtadee_Billah_in_2024.jpg)](/wiki/File:Family_of_Crown_Prince_Al-Muhtadee_Billah_in_2024.jpg)

Al-Muhtadee Billah and his family at an [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") [friendly match](/wiki/Exhibition_game "Exhibition game") in 2024

He is a [pool](/wiki/Pocket_billiards "Pocket billiards") and [snooker](/wiki/Snooker "Snooker") enthusiast, and participated in the [2006 WPA Men's World Nine-ball Championship](/wiki/2006_WPA_Men%27s_World_Nine-ball_Championship "2006 WPA Men's World Nine-ball Championship"), and later the [2008 WPA World Eight-ball Championship](/wiki/2008_WPA_World_Eight-ball_Championship "2008 WPA World Eight-ball Championship").[[48]](#cite_note-pro9_The2-48) The 2007 [World Pool Championship](/wiki/WPA_World_Nine-ball_Championship "WPA World Nine-ball Championship") was launched on 10 October at the [Sofitel](/wiki/Sofitel "Sofitel") Philippine Plaza Hotel in [Manila](/wiki/Manila "Manila"); Al-Muhtadee Billah represented Brunei in the tournament at the [Araneta Coliseum](/wiki/Araneta_Coliseum "Araneta Coliseum") from 3 to 11 November.[[49]](#cite_note-49)[[50]](#cite_note-50)

A football club, the [Brunei DPMM FC](/wiki/Brunei_DPMM_FC "Brunei DPMM FC"), is owned by Prince Al-Muhtadee Billah, who previously played as a goalkeeper for the team.[[51]](#cite_note-51)

Al-Muhtadee Billah also has a fleet of vehicles that includes a [Ferrari 599 GTB](/wiki/Ferrari_599_GTB "Ferrari 599 GTB"), [Mercedes McLaren SLR](/wiki/Mercedes_McLaren_SLR "Mercedes McLaren SLR"), and [Lamborghini Murcielago LP640](/wiki/Lamborghini_Murcielago_LP640 "Lamborghini Murcielago LP640"). Additionally, he is reported to be quiet and religious.[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4)

### Marriage and children

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Al-Muhtadee_Billah&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Marriage and children")]

On 9 September 2004, Al-Muhtadee Billah married 17-year-old [Pengiran Anak Sarah](/wiki/Sarah,_Crown_Princess_of_Brunei "Sarah, Crown Princess of Brunei") at Istana Nurul Iman in Bandar Seri Begawan.[[52]](#cite_note-wedding-52) Guests included the [Duke of Gloucester](/wiki/Prince_Richard,_Duke_of_Gloucester "Prince Richard, Duke of Gloucester"), the [Crown Prince of Japan](/wiki/Naruhito "Naruhito"), the [Yang di-Pertuan Agong of Malaysia](/wiki/Sirajuddin_of_Perlis "Sirajuddin of Perlis"), Princes [Bandar bin Sultan](/wiki/Bandar_bin_Sultan_Al_Saud "Bandar bin Sultan Al Saud") and [Saud bin Faisal of Saudi Arabia](/wiki/Saud_bin_Faisal_Al_Saud "Saud bin Faisal Al Saud"), the [King of Bahrain](/wiki/Hamad_bin_Isa_Al_Khalifa "Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa"), and several Malaysian sultans.[[52]](#cite_note-wedding-52) The wedding was also attended by heads of state and government from Singapore, Thailand, Malaysia, Indonesia, and the Philippines.[[52]](#cite_note-wedding-52) The wedding included a *bersanding* ceremony, and a drive around Bandar Seri Begawan in a golden-topped [Rolls-Royce](/wiki/Rolls-Royce_Motors "Rolls-Royce Motors").[[53]](#cite_note-53)

The royal couple have four children. Their first child and future heir to the Brunei throne, [Prince Abdul Muntaqim](/wiki/Prince_Abdul_Muntaqim_of_Brunei "Prince Abdul Muntaqim of Brunei"), was born on 17 March 2007 at the [Raja Isteri Pengiran Anak Saleha Hospital](/wiki/Raja_Isteri_Pengiran_Anak_Saleha_Hospital "Raja Isteri Pengiran Anak Saleha Hospital").[[54]](#cite_note-54)[[55]](#cite_note-55) Their second child, a daughter, [Pengiran Anak Muneerah Madhul Bolkiah](/wiki/Princess_Muneerah_of_Brunei "Princess Muneerah of Brunei"), was born on 2 January 2011.[[56]](#cite_note-name-56) Pengiran Anak Sarah gave birth to their third child and second son, [Pengiran Muda Muhammad Aiman](/wiki/Prince_Muhammad_Aiman_of_Brunei "Prince Muhammad Aiman of Brunei") on 7 June 2015.[[57]](#cite_note-57) Their fourth child and second daughter, Pengiran Anak Faathimah Az-Zahraa' Raihaanul Bolkiah, was born at 15:54, on 1 December 2017.[[58]](#cite_note-58)

On 4 September 2006, during the 18th [convocation ceremony](/wiki/Convocation_ceremony "Convocation ceremony") of UBD, Al-Muhtadee Billah received an [honorary doctorate](/wiki/Honorary_Doctorate "Honorary Doctorate") in [literature](/wiki/Literature "Literature") from his father, Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah.[[18]](#cite_note-:8-18) At the 11th UTB convocation ceremony on 14 September 2023, his father awarded Al-Muhtadee Billah an honorary doctorate in [technology innovation](/wiki/Technology_innovation "Technology innovation") in appreciation of his contributions to UTB's growth as the academic leader in engineering, business, science, and technology since the university's upgrade and accreditation.[[65]](#cite_note-65) On 17 September 2008, the acting assistant rector of UNISSA granted him an honorary doctorate in [Islamic finance](/wiki/Islamic_finance "Islamic finance") in recognition of his leadership and contributions, as well as his noteworthy accomplishments in promoting the expansion, advancement, and empowerment of the Islamic finance ecosystem on a national and worldwide scale.[[66]](#cite_note-66)
